data.output:
1	# Visual Suite — Principles, Tools, User Workflow
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-29
4	Status: Draft v6 (locked; reviewers' Q1–Q6 resolved)
5	
6	> **Note (2026-05-01):** the original plan placed DESIGN.md at the repo root.
7	> The canonical location is now `.stitch/DESIGN.md`. References below to
8	> `DESIGN.md` / `/DESIGN.md` / "repo root" should be read as `.stitch/DESIGN.md`.
9	
10	## Summary
11	
12	DDx ships a coordinated graphical suite covering three lenses on the product:
13	**principles**, **tools**, and **user workflow**. Each lens gets per-element
14	prompts plus a composite; the user-workflow lens is the capstone.
15	
16	Visual style is established via Google's **DESIGN.md** format spec
17	(github.com/google-labs-code/design.md) — markdown + YAML frontmatter
18	describing tokens (colors, typography, spacing, components) plus prose
19	rationale. Comes with a `@google/design.md` npm CLI for `lint`, `diff`,
20	`export` (Tailwind/DTCG), and `spec`. **Status: alpha, version `0.1.0`.**
21	
22	Project standardizes on **bun/bunx** for Node-ecosystem tooling, matching
23	existing precedent (the SvelteKit frontend at `cli/internal/server/frontend/`
24	already uses Bun per CLAUDE.md). `package.json` is the canonical declaration
25	surface for npm-delivered tools — no DDx wrapper, no FEAT-024 spec, no
26	DDx-owned lockfile.
27	
28	All visuals are generated artifacts (per the artifact-and-run-architecture
29	plan), live in the helix phase tree alongside the docs they depict, with
30	sidecar metadata and `generated_by` provenance.
31	
32	## Architecture
33	
34	```
35	DESIGN.md authored at repo root
36	      ↓
37	DESIGN.md → website palette via Hextra theme overrides (spike — see V3.5)
38	DESIGN.md tokens → image-generation prompts (palette anchors)
39	      ↓
40	Visual generation (one at a time, $10 cap, pressure-test gated)
41	      ↓
42	┌────────────────────────────────┐
43	│ Principle prompts (×6)         │
44	│   ↓                            │
45	│ Principle composite (capstone) │
46	├────────────────────────────────┤
47	│ Tool prompts (×4)              │
48	│   ↓                            │
49	│ Tool graphics + composite      │
50	├────────────────────────────────┤
51	│ User-workflow diagram (capstone)
52	└────────────────────────────────┘
53	      ↓
54	Website redesign (beads to be created — 28, 29) consumes
55	```
56	
57	## The three lenses
58	
59	### Lens 1 — Principles (×6 prompts; composite ships; standalone optional)
60	
61	One prompt per thesis principle. Lever/load/fulcrum metaphor is load-bearing.
62	
63	| Principle | Visual concept |
64	|---|---|
65	| 1. Abstraction is the lever | The lever arm — multi-level stack with intent transmitting |
66	| 2. Software is iteration over tracked work | Cyclic motion of the lever — discrete tracked items |
67	| 3. Methodology is plural | Multiple lever handles / interchangeable grips |
68	| 4. LLMs are stochastic, unreliable, costly | The load — non-uniform, probabilistic mass |
69	| 5. Evidence provides memory | Trail / chain of receipts left by the moving lever |
70	| 6. Human-AI collaboration is the fulcrum | The pivot — humans and AI in contact at the seam |
71	
72	All 6 prompts authored regardless of render decision. Composite ships first
73	(pressure-test the metaphor); standalone renders are optional follow-up if
74	the composite reads well alone.
75	
76	### Lens 2 — Tools (×4 + composite — full per-element + composite)
77	
78	| Tool | Visual concept |
79	|---|---|
80	| Bead tracker | DAG with priority queue + ready/blocked states |
81	| Document graph (artifacts) | Multi-node graph with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges |
82	| Agentic execution (run/try/work) | Three-layer wrapping; worktree isolation visible |
83	| Plugins | Modular composition; HELIX/Dun snapping into shared core |
84	
85	### Lens 3 — User workflow (capstone)
86	
87	Iterative diagram showing how a user actually works with DDx over time:
88	author/refine artifact → graph synthesizes context → bead created → agent
89	runs (`ddx try` / `ddx work`) → evidence captured → human re-aligns → loop
90	closes. Explanatory register; load-bearing artifact.
91	
92	## Storage — helix phase tree
93	
94	Visuals live alongside the docs they depict. DESIGN.md is the cross-cutting
95	exception at repo root.
96	
97	```
98	DESIGN.md                                          ← cross-cutting; repo root
99	package.json                                       ← repo root (bun-managed)
100	bun.lock                                           ← repo root (text lockfile)
101	docs/helix/00-discover/
102	├── product-vision.md
103	└── visuals/
104	    ├── principle-1-lever.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
105	    ├── ... (×6 principles)
106	    └── principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
107	docs/helix/01-frame/
108	├── prd.md
109	└── visuals/
110	    ├── tool-tracker.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
111	    ├── tool-doc-graph.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
112	    ├── tool-execution.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
113	    ├── tool-plugins.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
114	    ├── tools-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
115	    └── user-workflow.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}
116	```
117	
118	Each visual artifact has `depends_on` pointing to the source doc and
119	`generated_by` pointing to its prompt source. First real test of
120	`generated_by` edges across the helix tree.
121	
122	## DESIGN.md is the visual style brief
123	
124	DESIGN.md *is* the style brief — its prose sections (Overview, Do's/Don'ts)
125	carry metaphor system and composition rules; its YAML carries tokens. No
126	separate STYLE.md needed.
127	
128	Two consumers:
129	- **Website (Hugo + Hextra):** spike palette injection via Hextra's
130	  `params.theme` / CSS variables (V3.5). Hextra ships precompiled CSS via
131	  Hugo Pipes; project-level Tailwind config consumption is unverified.
132	  Fall back to accept-divergence (DESIGN.md drives prompts only) if
133	  Hextra reach is insufficient.
134	- **Image prompts:** prompts reference DESIGN.md palette by hex and named
135	  tokens for cross-prompt consistency anchor.
136	
137	Repo placement: `/DESIGN.md` (brand-level artifact for the whole product).
138	
139	## Tool dependency declaration: `package.json` (no DDx wrapper)
140	
141	Standard Node convention. No FEAT-024 spec, no DDx-owned lockfile, no
142	`ddx tool run` wrapper.
143	
144	```bash
145	bun add --dev @google/design.md@0.1.0      # adds to package.json + bun.lock
146	bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md      # standard invocation
147	```
148	
149	`ddx doctor` extension scans known `package.json` locations (root,
150	`website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`) and reports `bun install` if
151	`node_modules/` is missing or out of date. `ddx init` summary mentions
152	discovered `package.json` files.
153	
154	DDx never wraps `bunx`; users invoke npm-ecosystem tools directly via
155	standard `bunx <pkg>` commands.
156	
157	## Generation strategy
158	
159	- **Generator:** `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` (Gemini 3 Pro Image preview)
160	  by default.
161	- **Each visual is a `generate-artifact` run** per the artifact-and-run plan.
162	- **`SYSTEM_TEMPLATE` override** for sober/utilitarian style (default
163	  pulls toward "dreamy illustration").
164	- **DESIGN.md tokens injected** into every prompt for palette/typography
165	  consistency.
166	- **Sequential rendering with $10 total budget cap and $1.50/image halt
167	  trigger.** Render one visual at a time; evaluate against acceptance
168	  criteria before generating the next. Halt on either cap. Per-image
169	  cap prevents one runaway prompt eating the budget.
170	- **First real dogfood** of the artifact infrastructure (sidecar I/O,
171	  `generated_by` edges, `ddx artifact regenerate` CLI). Bugs surfaced here
172	  feed back into beads `ddx-d5e71fb3` (FEAT-010), `ddx-5d92b873` (FEAT-007),
173	  and the artifact-regenerate implementation.
174	- **Regen policy:** every visual `depends_on` DESIGN.md and its prompt
175	  source. Either-source change marks the visual stale via the separate
176	  `generated_by` staleness rule. Regen is explicit (`ddx artifact regenerate`),
177	  not automatic — operator decides when to spend tokens.
178	
179	## Acceptance criteria per visual
180	
181	- Renders cleanly at intended placement (homepage hero, docs page, README)
182	- Contrast OK in light and dark mode
183	- Mobile crop preserves the message
184	- Alt text drafted and in sidecar
185	- File size budget under 200KB (compress / downsize as needed)
186	- Metaphor parses without explanatory copy
187	- **Brand-fit: sober/utilitarian register matching DDx's docs surface; no
188	  AI-gloss, no kitsch, no dreamy illustration**
189	
190	## Risks
191	
192	- **DESIGN.md is alpha** (`0.1.0`). Pin exact `0.1.0`; budget for both
193	  format spec AND `--format tailwind` exporter breaking on bumps (two
194	  alpha surfaces stack).
195	- **Hextra Tailwind path unverified.** V3.5 is a spike. Expected outcome:
196	  palette override via Hextra theme params works; if not, fall back to
197	  accept-divergence rather than fork Hextra.
198	- **Cross-prompt style consistency** on `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` is
199	  unproven. DESIGN.md tokens anchor palette but composition consistency
200	  across 6+ subsections is genuine risk. Composite-first sequencing
201	  (V6 before V7) limits exposure.
202	- **Metaphor strain when rendered.** Pressure-test in V6 before committing
203	  further. If lever/fulcrum/load doesn't carry, revise the visual concept
204	  (not principle wording).
205	- **Artifact-infra bugs** could block visual generation. Plan accepts
206	  iteration: bugs → fixes in artifact-and-run beads → resume.
207	- **Cost overrun.** $10 cap is for the full run; halt at cap and reassess.
208	  Sequential generation gives visibility; cache-on-prompt-hash prevents
209	  accidental re-spending.
210	- **Two alpha surfaces.** DESIGN.md format and exporter both alpha.
211	  Pinning `0.1.0` exactly mitigates but doesn't eliminate risk; budget
212	  ~half a day per bump for migration.
213	
214	## Bead breakdown
215	
216	### Tooling foundation (gates the rest)
217	
218	- **V2.** Extend `ddx doctor` (and optionally `ddx init` summary) to detect
219	  `package.json` at known locations (root, `website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`)
220	  and report `bun install` if `node_modules/` is missing or stale. ~100 LoC.
221	
222	### DESIGN.md integration
223	
224	- **V3.** Author `DESIGN.md` at repo root. Capture existing precedent
225	  (logo, current rgba triad, terminal-demo tone). Tokens (colors,
226	  typography, spacing, components); prose Overview, Do's/Don'ts, metaphor
227	  system. Run `bun add --dev @google/design.md@0.1.0` at repo root; lint
228	  with `bunx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md`. **Treat as foundation,
229	  not throwaway spike** — DDx has no visual language today; embedding
230	  DESIGN.md is correct. Adapt as the format evolves.
231	- **V3.5.** Wire DESIGN.md → website (Hugo + Hextra) palette. Try Hextra
232	  `params.theme` / CSS-variable override path first. Fall back to
233	  accept-divergence (DESIGN.md drives image prompts only) if Hextra
234	  reach insufficient. Document outcome in bead resolution.
235	- **V3.6.** Wire DESIGN.md → frontend (SvelteKit + Tailwind v4) at
236	  `cli/internal/server/frontend/tailwind.config.js`. Use
237	  `bunx @google/design.md export --format tailwind` to produce a
238	  theme JSON the frontend's tailwind.config.js consumes. Easier path
239	  than Hextra (frontend already uses Tailwind); ensures DESIGN.md ROI
240	  even if V3.5 falls back to accept-divergence. **Goal: align on a new
241	  visual language across the entire product surface.**
242	- **V4.** Lefthook + CI lint integration: `bunx @google/design.md lint
243	  DESIGN.md` runs on commit if DESIGN.md is modified.
244	
245	### Principles lens
246	
247	- **V5.** Author all 6 principle-graphic prompts at
248	  `docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/principle-N-*.prompt.md`.
249	- **V6.** Author principle-composite prompt + generate **(first render —
250	  pressure-test gate; budget tracking starts here)**. If the lever/fulcrum/
251	  load metaphor doesn't render usefully, pause and revise before proceeding.
252	  **Hard-blocked on artifact infrastructure**: beads `ddx-d5e71fb3` (FEAT-010
253	  three-layer substrate), `ddx-5d92b873` (FEAT-007 sidecar + generated_by),
254	  and `ddx artifact regenerate` CLI implementation must be merged first.
255	  No manual prompt-file → image fallback; if those slip, V6 slips.
256	- **V7** *(optional, conditional on V6 outcome).* Render the 6 principle
257	  subsections standalone. Skip if composite suffices or if cross-prompt
258	  consistency fails.
259	
260	### Tools lens
261	
262	- **V8.** Author 4 tool-graphic prompts at `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/`.
263	- **V9.** Generate 4 tool graphics + tool composite **(sequential, budget-tracked)**.
264	
265	### Workflow capstone
266	
267	- **V10.** Author user-workflow prompt + generate **(sequential,
268	  budget-tracked)**. Explanatory register; load-bearing.
269	
270	### Website integration (downstream — beads not yet created)
271	
272	- Replace 6 feature cards on landing with 6 principle cards.
273	- Add Why DDx top-nav page (exposition around principles).
274	- Concept "Why this exists" callouts.
275	- Replace README tagline with thesis.
276	- E2E test updates.
277	- Visual integration consumes V6/V9/V10 outputs.
278	
279	## Reviewer ask
280	
281	1. **Tool-declaration simplification** — `package.json` + `ddx doctor`
282	   detection only, no FEAT-024, no DDx wrapper. Right reduction, or did we
283	   lose something load-bearing?
284	2. **bun standardization** — repo already uses Bun for the frontend per
285	   CLAUDE.md. Any subdirectory or build step that breaks if root tooling
286	   moves to bun (e.g., CI assumed npm)?
287	3. **Multiple `package.json` scan in `ddx doctor`** — known set (root,
288	   `website/`, `cli/internal/server/frontend/`) vs glob discovery? Static
289	   list is simpler; glob risks scanning user output dirs.
290	4. **`package.json` placement for `@google/design.md`** — root chosen for
291	   cross-cutting linter. Concerns about polluting repo root with a
292	   `package.json`/`node_modules` when most of the project is Go?
293	5. **Hextra spike fallback (accept-divergence)** — if Hextra can't consume
294	   DESIGN.md tokens, website CSS stays Hextra-default and DESIGN.md drives
295	   image prompts only. Is image-prompt-only consumption sufficient ROI to
296	   justify the DESIGN.md effort?
297	6. **Cost cap $10 sequential** — realistic for 11+ Gemini-3-Pro-Image
298	   generations at 2K? Should we set a per-image cost ceiling too?
299	7. **Acceptance criteria** — anything missing? Brand-fit / kitsch judgment?
300	8. **Alpha-stack risk** — two alpha surfaces (DESIGN.md format + exporter).
301	   Sufficient to pin `0.1.0`, or should we wait until v1?
302	9. **Helix-tree storage of visuals** — `docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/`
303	   and `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/`. Anti-patterns or convention violations?
304	10. **Anything missing.**
305	
306	Codebase context:
307	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md`
308	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/CLAUDE.md` (bun precedent)
309	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/website/` (Hugo + Hextra; no Tailwind today)
310	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/cli/internal/server/frontend/` (existing bun/SvelteKit setup)
311	- `/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`
312	- DESIGN.md spec: github.com/google-labs-code/design.md
313	
314	Respond with sections (omit empty):
315	**Keep:** correct, load-bearing
316	**Cut:** remove
317	**Missing:** gaps
318	**Risks:** verification needed (cite file:line)
319	**Questions:** require USER input
320	
321	Concise. 2 sentences per finding. Severity-ordered.
322

1	---
2	title: "Tool — Agentic execution (run / try / work)"
3	tool: "Agentic execution"
4	component: "Three-layer wrapping with worktree isolation"
5	generator: nano-banana-pro-openrouter
6	model: gemini-3-pro-image-preview
7	aspect_ratio: "16:9"
8	size: "2K"
9	depends_on:
10	  - .stitch/DESIGN.md
11	  - docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
12	---
13	
14	# Prompt
15	
16	A precise technical diagram showing three nested rounded-rectangle layers, viewed straight on as an engineering schematic — concentric containers stepping inward from outermost to innermost, illustrating how `ddx work` wraps `ddx try` which wraps `ddx run`. Each layer is labeled at the top-left of its bounding rectangle in **Inter 0.75rem weight 500**, with a short subtitle in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280** beneath:
17	
18	- **Outer layer — `ddx work`** (queue drain). Stroke 1.5px **#4878C6** (lever blue), fill **#F9FAFB**. Subtitle: "drain the bead queue". On the left edge of this outer layer, a small priority-queue column shows three stacked blue rounded rectangles labeled **DDX-001**, **DDX-002**, **DDX-003**, with a thin **#4878C6** arrow exiting the queue and entering the middle layer, indicating beads are pulled one at a time.
19	- **Middle layer — `ddx try` (`execute-bead`)** (worktree-isolated attempt). Stroke 1.5px **#4878C6**, fill **#FFFFFF**. Subtitle: "isolated worktree • merge or discard". Inside this layer, a clearly delineated **worktree boundary** is drawn as a dashed 1.5px **#6B7280** rounded rectangle labeled `.execute-bead-wt-…` in **JetBrains Mono 0.75rem #6B7280**, visually communicating filesystem isolation. A small fork-and-merge glyph on the right edge of this layer (two thin **#4878C6** lines branching out and rejoining) represents the merge-or-discard outcome.
20	- **Inner layer — `ddx run`** (a single agent invocation). Stroke 1.5px **#4878C6**, fill **#FFFFFF**. Subtitle: "one agent call". Contains a small node labeled **agent** in **Inter 0.75rem #111827** with a thin **#4878C6** arrow looping into and out of it, indicating the actual model invocation.
21	
22	To the right of the diagram, a thin **#E5E7EB** evidence rail shows three small stacked document glyphs (rounded rectangles, 1.5px **#6B7280** stroke) labeled `bundle/`, `result/`, `commit` in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**, with thin **#8E35A3** (load purple) dashed `generated_by` edges pointing back into the middle layer — indicating execution evidence is captured per-attempt.
23	
24	Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB**. Tone: technical reference manual, systems-architecture diagram drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian.
25	
26	# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)
27	
28	- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** for layer strokes and flow arrows; load purple **#8E35A3** only on `generated_by` evidence edges; neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#F9FAFB**, **#FFFFFF**. Fulcrum green **#35A35F** is not used in this image.
29	- Typography: Inter for layer labels and subtitles, JetBrains Mono for the worktree path and command names. Label scale (0.75rem) only. No display type.
30	- Register: technical schematic, systems-architecture diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.
31	- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particles, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
32	- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters, no robot avatars for "agent").
33	- Metaphor must parse without caption: three nested layers with the middle layer visibly isolated as a worktree.
34	
35	# Negative prompt
36	
37	3D render, photographic realism, gradient backgrounds, neon, glow, bokeh, depth-of-field blur, particles, sparkles, cinematic lighting, painterly illustration, hand-drawn sketch style, watercolor, isometric video-game aesthetic, robots, brains, circuit boards, cables, screens, characters, faces, hands, marketing-deck composition, terminal-window mockups, command-line screenshots, chat bubbles, gear icons.
38	
39	# Acceptance criteria
40	
41	- Reads as three nested execution layers with `work` outermost, `try` in the middle (visibly worktree-isolated), and `run` innermost — without explanatory caption.
42	- Lever blue **#4878C6** dominates the layer strokes and flow; load purple **#8E35A3** appears only on evidence/`generated_by` edges; the worktree boundary is visibly distinct (dashed) from solid layer strokes.
43	- Sober/utilitarian register; no AI-gloss.
44	- Light/dark mode contrast OK; mobile crop preserves the metaphor.
45	- File size ≤ 200KB after compression.
46

1	---
2	title: "Tool — Document graph (artifacts)"
3	tool: "Document graph"
4	component: "Multi-node graph with depends_on and generated_by edges"
5	generator: nano-banana-pro-openrouter
6	model: gemini-3-pro-image-preview
7	aspect_ratio: "16:9"
8	size: "2K"
9	depends_on:
10	  - .stitch/DESIGN.md
11	  - docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
12	---
13	
14	# Prompt
15	
16	A precise technical diagram of a multi-node document graph rendered as an engineering schematic, viewed straight on. Roughly ten document nodes are arrayed across the canvas in a layered layout: source artifacts on the left (vision, PRD, feature specs), derived artifacts in the middle (design plans, beads), and generated artifacts on the right (visuals, reports). Each node is a small rounded rectangle (rounded corner radius 8px), filled **#FFFFFF** with a 1.5px stroke **#111827**, labeled in **Inter 0.75rem weight 500 #111827** with a short doc name like `vision.md`, `prd.md`, `tool-tracker.png`, `bead-DDX-001`.
17	
18	Two distinct edge kinds connect the nodes, with a small legend on the bottom edge:
19	- **`depends_on` edge** — solid 1.5px line **#4878C6** (lever blue) with a small filled-triangle arrowhead. Connects a derived doc to the upstream source it relies on.
20	- **`generated_by` edge** — dashed 1.5px line **#8E35A3** (load purple) with a small open-triangle arrowhead. Connects a generated artifact (image, report) back to the prompt or template that produced it.
21	
22	Edges flow generally left-to-right but cross layers where needed, showing the network nature of the graph rather than a strict tree. A small subset of nodes on the right are clearly **generated** (e.g., `principles-composite.png`, `tool-tracker.png`) and have both a blue `depends_on` edge to a source doc *and* a purple dashed `generated_by` edge to a `.prompt.md` node, illustrating the dual-edge model.
23	
24	Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB**. Bottom legend strip shows two short edge samples — a solid blue line labeled "depends_on" and a dashed purple line labeled "generated_by" — in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**. Tone: technical reference manual, knowledge-graph schematic drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian.
25	
26	# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)
27	
28	- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** for `depends_on` edges, load purple **#8E35A3** for `generated_by` edges; neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#FFFFFF** only. Fulcrum green **#35A35F** is not used in this image.
29	- Typography: Inter for labels, regular/medium weight, label scale (0.75rem). JetBrains Mono permitted for filename labels if it improves legibility. No display type.
30	- Register: technical schematic, graph-theory diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.
31	- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particles, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
32	- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters).
33	- Metaphor must parse without caption: a directed graph of documents with two distinct edge kinds (dependency vs. provenance).
34	
35	# Negative prompt
36	
37	3D render, photographic realism, gradient backgrounds, neon, glow, bokeh, depth-of-field blur, particles, sparkles, cinematic lighting, painterly illustration, hand-drawn sketch style, watercolor, isometric video-game aesthetic, robots, brains, circuit boards, cables, screens, characters, faces, hands, marketing-deck composition, file-folder icons, paper-stack illustrations, cloud icons.
38	
39	# Acceptance criteria
40	
41	- Reads as a graph of documents with two clearly distinct edge kinds (depends_on vs. generated_by) without explanatory caption.
42	- Lever blue **#4878C6** appears only on solid `depends_on` edges; load purple **#8E35A3** appears only on dashed `generated_by` edges.
43	- Sober/utilitarian register; no AI-gloss.
44	- Light/dark mode contrast OK; mobile crop preserves the metaphor.
45	- File size ≤ 200KB after compression.
46

input.description: Check principles visuals and recent executions

Decision: no_changes. The plan (docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md
§"Bead breakdown" V6) explicitly forbids a manual prompt-file → image fallback:
"No manual prompt-file → image fallback; if those slip, V6 slips." A well-justified
no_changes is preferable to authoring a prompt and shelling out to an image tool
outside the FEAT-010 substrate.

What is done
- Verified the named blocker beads are closed: ddx-d5e71fb3 (FEAT-010 three-layer
  substrate), ddx-5d92b873 (FEAT-007 sidecar + generated_by), ddx-64fbb1b3
  (`ddx artifact regenerate` CLI).
- Verified `ddx artifact regenerate` exists and dispatches into the FEAT-010
  store via cli/cmd/artifact.go: it requires (a) the artifact present in the
  document graph and (b) at least one execution definition that lists the
  artifact under produces_artifact.
- Read the six existing principle prompts under docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/
  (principle-1-lever … principle-6-fulcrum) — V5 author-prompts work is in
  place and can be referenced by the composite prompt.

What is blocking
1. Missing image-generation skill. The `nano-banana-pro-openrouter` skill is
   referenced from `.crush/skills/` and `.pi/skills/` as symlinks pointing to
   `.agents/skills/nano-banana-pro-openrouter/`, but that target directory does
   not exist in this worktree (only helix-* skills and `ddx` are under
   `.agents/skills/`). Without the skill the generator cannot run.
2. No execution definition registered. `ddx exec list --json` returns `[]`;
   there is no nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator definition, and no
   definition lists `principles-composite` (or any visual artifact id) under
   produces_artifact. `ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite` therefore
   fails with "artifact \"principles-composite\" not found in document graph"
   — the artifact is not registered, and even if it were, selectArtifactGenerator
   in cli/cmd/artifact.go:99 would error with "no generator definition or
   generated_by provenance".
3. Sidecar metadata not authored. The plan calls for
   `principles-composite.{prompt.md,png,png.ddx.yaml}` co-located with the V5
   prompts, with `depends_on` and `generated_by` edges. The `.ddx.yaml` sidecar
   for the (yet-unrendered) composite cannot be written without a real
   `generated_by` run record, which only the substrate run produces.

What a follow-up attempt would need (in order)
1. A predecessor bead that installs / vendors the `nano-banana-pro-openrouter`
   skill into `.agents/skills/` so the broken symlinks resolve. Today this
   skill is missing from the execute-bead worktree.
2. A predecessor bead that authors a `ddx exec define` execution definition
   for the nano-banana-pro-openrouter generator with
   `produces_artifact: principles-composite` (and, ideally, the six standalone
   principle artifact IDs as well so V7 can reuse it).
3. A predecessor bead that registers the `principles-composite` artifact in
   the document graph (sidecar with id, depends_on .stitch/DESIGN.md and the
   six principle prompts, media_type image/png) so `ddx artifact regenerate`
   resolves it.

Once those three preconditions hold, V6 collapses to: author
docs/helix/00-discover/visuals/principles-composite.prompt.md (referencing the
six existing principle prompts and DESIGN.md tokens), then run
`ddx artifact regenerate principles-composite`. The composite prompt should
explicitly assemble the lever metaphor — beam (P1), cyclic motion (P2),
interchangeable handles (P3), non-uniform load (P4), receipts trail (P5),
fulcrum at the human-AI seam (P6) — so the pressure-test reads on the first
render. Budget tracking ($10 cap, $1.50/image halt) starts when the substrate
run actually executes.

Recommendation: file three predecessor beads above (skill-install, exec-define,
artifact-register), block ddx-ea3a7e23 on them, and re-queue. Do not escalate
to a stronger model — model capability is not the constraint here.",

2	title: "Tool — Bead tracker"
3	tool: "Bead tracker"
4	component: "DAG with priority queue + ready/blocked states"
5	generator: nano-banana-pro-openrouter
6	model: gemini-3-pro-image-preview
7	aspect_ratio: "16:9"
8	size: "2K"
9	depends_on:
10	  - .stitch/DESIGN.md
11	  - docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
12	---
13	
14	# Prompt
15	
16	A precise technical diagram of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of work items, viewed straight on as an engineering schematic. Roughly twelve small rounded-rectangle nodes (rounded corner radius 8px) are laid out left-to-right across four implicit columns; each node is labeled with a short id like **DDX-001**, **DDX-002**, etc. in **Inter, 0.75rem, weight 500**, color **#111827**. Directed arrows in **#6B7280** connect parent nodes to dependent children, all flowing left to right; arrowheads are small filled triangles, not gradients.
17	
18	Three node states are visually distinguished by fill, with a small legend along the bottom edge:
19	- **Ready** — solid fill **#4878C6** (lever blue) with white label text **#FFFFFF**, indicating no unmet dependencies.
20	- **Blocked** — outlined only, 1.5px stroke **#6B7280** on a **#FFFFFF** fill, label in **#6B7280**, indicating an unmet upstream dependency.
21	- **Done** — solid fill **#35A35F** (fulcrum green) with white label text, with a thin checkmark glyph in **#FFFFFF**.
22	
23	On the left edge of the canvas, a vertical **priority queue** column is depicted: a tall thin rounded rectangle on **#F9FAFB** surface containing the ready nodes (blue) stacked top-to-bottom in priority order, with a small numeric "1, 2, 3" rank in **#6B7280** at each slot's left margin. A thin **#4878C6** arrow exits the top of the queue and points right into the wider DAG, indicating "next bead pulled from the queue."
24	
25	Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB** for engineering-drawing register. A thin **#E5E7EB** legend strip along the bottom shows three small swatches (Ready / Blocked / Done) labeled in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**. Tone: technical reference manual, project-management diagram drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian.
26	
27	# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)
28	
29	- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** for ready/active, fulcrum green **#35A35F** for done, neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#F9FAFB**, **#FFFFFF** only. No tertiary purple in this image.
30	- Typography: Inter for all labels, regular/medium weight, label scale (0.75rem) only. JetBrains Mono permitted for the bead id text if it improves legibility. No display type, no decorative text.
31	- Register: technical schematic, project-management diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration.
32	- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particles, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
33	- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters, no avatars).
34	- Metaphor must parse without caption: a DAG of work items with a priority queue feeding ready beads into execution.
35	
36	# Negative prompt
37	
38	3D render, photographic realism, gradient backgrounds, neon, glow, bokeh, depth-of-field blur, particles, sparkles, cinematic lighting, painterly illustration, hand-drawn sketch style, watercolor, isometric video-game aesthetic, robots, brains, circuit boards, cables, screens, characters, faces, hands, marketing-deck composition, kanban board mockups, sticky notes, chat bubbles, gears.
39	
40	# Acceptance criteria
41	
42	- Reads as a DAG of tracked work items with a priority queue and three clear states without explanatory caption.
43	- Lever blue **#4878C6** dominates ready/active items; fulcrum green **#35A35F** appears only on completed nodes.
44	- Sober/utilitarian register; no AI-gloss.
45	- Light/dark mode contrast OK; mobile crop preserves the metaphor.
46	- File size ≤ 200KB after compression.
47

input.content:
---
title: "User workflow — iterative loop with DDx"
lens: "User workflow (capstone)"
component: "Six-stage closed loop: artifact → graph → bead → agent → evidence → re-align"
generator: nano-banana-pro-openrouter
model: gemini-3-pro-image-preview
aspect_ratio: "16:9"
size: "2K"
depends_on:
  - .stitch/DESIGN.md
  - docs/helix/01-frame/prd.md
  - docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/tool-tracker.prompt.md
  - docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/tool-doc-graph.prompt.md
  - docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/tool-execution.prompt.md
  - docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/tool-plugins.prompt.md
---

# Prompt

A precise technical diagram of a closed iterative workflow rendered as an engineering schematic, viewed straight on. The composition is a single horizontal **16:9** canvas with a clockwise circular loop occupying the center, formed by six numbered stages connected by directional **#4878C6** (lever blue) flow arrows with small filled-triangle arrowheads. The loop reads as a complete cycle: stages flow ① → ② → ③ → ④ → ⑤ → ⑥ → back to ① with no terminal arrow, communicating that the user re-enters the loop continuously rather than finishing.

Each stage is a rounded rectangle (rounded corner radius 8px) of equal size, **#FFFFFF** fill, 1.5px **#111827** stroke, with a small numbered badge in the top-left corner — a **#4878C6** filled circle 18px diameter containing a white numeral in **Inter 0.75rem weight 600 #FFFFFF**. Inside each stage card, the stage name is set in **Inter 0.875rem weight 500 #111827** with a one-line subtitle beneath in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**, and a small inline glyph echoes that stage's tool from the existing tool lens (consistent shorthand, not a re-render):

- **① Author / refine artifact** — subtitle: "human edits a markdown doc". Glyph: a small document rectangle with two faint horizontal text lines, 1.5px **#111827** stroke. Stage card sits at the **9 o'clock** position. The author here is a human; the input is a `.md` file in the helix tree.
- **② Graph synthesizes context** — subtitle: "depends_on resolves upstream". Glyph: three small connected nodes with two solid **#4878C6** edges between them (a miniature document graph). Stage card sits at **11 o'clock**. The graph walks `depends_on` to assemble the artifact's effective context.
- **③ Bead created** — subtitle: "tracked work item enters the queue". Glyph: a small DAG snippet with one solid **#4878C6** node (ready) and one **#6B7280** outlined node (blocked). Stage card sits at **1 o'clock**. The bead carries an id like **DDX-042** rendered in **JetBrains Mono 0.6875rem #6B7280** beneath the glyph.
- **④ Agent runs** — subtitle: "`ddx try` / `ddx work` in worktree". Stage card sits at **3 o'clock** and is visibly the most active node: stroke 1.5px **#4878C6** instead of **#111827**, fill **#F9FAFB**. Glyph: three concentric rounded rectangles (the three-layer wrapping from the execution lens), with the innermost containing a tiny **agent** label in **Inter 0.625rem #111827**. A dashed 1.5px **#6B7280** boundary around the inner two layers indicates the worktree.
- **⑤ Evidence captured** — subtitle: "bundle, result, commit". Glyph: a small evidence rail of three stacked document rectangles with a thin **#8E35A3** (load purple) dashed `generated_by` tick on the right edge. Stage card sits at **5 o'clock**.
- **⑥ Human re-aligns** — subtitle: "review evidence, refine artifact". Stage card sits at **7 o'clock** and is rendered with a thin **#35A35F** (fulcrum green) inner accent line — a 1px stroke offset 4px inside the stage rectangle — indicating the human-AI seam closes here. Glyph: a small fulcrum triangle in **#35A35F** beneath a horizontal beam segment, echoing the principles lens. The arrow leaving stage ⑥ points back to stage ① to visibly close the loop.

A thin **#8E35A3** dashed `generated_by` edge runs from stage ⑤ inward to a small **commit** glyph at the loop's center (a tiny **#111827** stroked rounded rectangle labeled `commit` in **JetBrains Mono 0.6875rem**), and from that center glyph two thin **#6B7280** dashed lines fan back to stages ① and ② — communicating that captured evidence updates the artifacts and the graph for the next iteration. The center glyph is small (~10% of canvas height) and does not compete with the loop ring.

The bottom edge of the canvas carries a **legend strip** in **#E5E7EB** at 4% opacity background with three short swatches in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280**:
- A solid **#4878C6** arrow labeled "flow"
- A dashed **#8E35A3** line labeled "generated_by"
- A **#35A35F** fulcrum triangle labeled "human-AI seam"

The top-left corner carries a small heading in **Inter 0.875rem weight 600 #111827** reading **"Working with DDx"** with a one-line subhead in **Inter 0.75rem #6B7280** reading **"the loop closes; the artifact improves"**. No other display type, no marketing tagline.

Background: flat **#FFFFFF** with subtle 4%-opacity grid registration marks in **#E5E7EB**. Tone: technical reference manual, systems-workflow diagram drawn as a patent figure, sober and utilitarian, explanatory register suitable for a load-bearing homepage hero or PRD figure.

# Style constraints (DESIGN.md)

- Palette: lever blue **#4878C6** dominates the loop arrows and ready/active accents; load purple **#8E35A3** appears only on dashed `generated_by` edges (stage ⑤ tick and the center fan-out); fulcrum green **#35A35F** appears only on stage ⑥'s inner accent line and the legend's seam glyph. Neutrals **#111827**, **#6B7280**, **#E5E7EB**, **#F9FAFB**, **#FFFFFF** elsewhere.
- Typography: Inter for stage names, subtitles, headings, and legend; JetBrains Mono for bead ids, the `commit` label, and other code-shaped tokens. Label scale (0.75rem) for subtitles, 0.875rem for stage names and the corner heading; no display type.
- Register: technical schematic, workflow diagram. Treat as a patent figure, not an illustration. Capstone of the visual suite — must read alongside the four tool diagrams without aesthetic discontinuity.
- No gradient washes, glows, lens flares, particles, dreamy lighting, or AI-art treatments.
- No anthropomorphism (no hands, no faces, no characters, no avatars for "human" or "agent" — the human-AI seam is communicated by the fulcrum glyph and the green accent only).
- The numbered loop must read clockwise, must close (no broken or open arc), and must carry six and only six stages. No additional decorative nodes.
- Metaphor must parse without caption: a closed iterative loop with the human re-entering at the seam and evidence flowing inward to update artifacts.

# Negative prompt

3D render, photographic realism, gradient backgrounds, neon, glow, bokeh, depth-of-field blur, particles, sparkles, cinematic lighting, painterly illustration, hand-drawn sketch style, watercolor, isometric video-game aesthetic, robots, brains, circuit boards, cables, screens, characters, faces, hands, body parts, marketing-deck composition, terminal-window mockups, command-line screenshots, chat bubbles, speech balloons, gears, infinity symbols, ouroboros snake, broken or open arcs that fail to close the loop, more than six numbered stages, fewer than six numbered stages, additional palette colors beyond the named tokens.

# Acceptance criteria

- Reads as a closed six-stage clockwise loop — author → graph → bead → agent → evidence → re-align → back to author — without explanatory caption.
- Stage ④ (agent runs) is visibly the most active node and shows the three-layer wrapping plus a worktree boundary echoing the execution lens.
- Stage ⑥ (human re-aligns) carries the fulcrum-green seam accent; no other stage uses fulcrum green.
- Lever blue **#4878C6** dominates the loop's flow arrows; load purple **#8E35A3** appears only on `generated_by` evidence edges; fulcrum green **#35A35F** appears only at the human-AI seam.
- Capstone reads as part of the visual suite — palette, line weights, label scale, and corner registration match the four tool diagrams (`tool-tracker`, `tool-doc-graph`, `tool-execution`, `tool-plugins`).
- Sober/utilitarian register; no AI-gloss, no kitsch.
- Light/dark mode contrast OK; mobile crop preserves the loop's closure and stage numbering.
- File size ≤ 200KB after compression.

input.command:
git add docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/user-workflow.prompt.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(visuals): author V10 user-workflow capstone prompt [ddx-76175851]

Authoring half of V10 per plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md. Six-stage
closed-loop diagram (author → graph → bead → agent → evidence →
re-align) tying the principles and tools lenses together; matches the
sober/utilitarian patent-figure register established by the tool prompts
and reuses the DESIGN.md token palette.

Generation half remains hard-blocked on the same artifact infrastructure
that closed V6 (ddx-ea3a7e23) and V9 (ddx-297e7b4b) as no_changes:
nano-banana-pro-openrouter skill missing under .agents/skills/, no
generator exec definition registered, and the user-workflow artifact is
not yet declared in the document graph. Plan forbids manual prompt-file
to image fallback (plan-2026-04-29-visual-suite.md V6 note), so render
must wait until those predecessors merge; this commit lands only the
authored prompt source.
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -20

data.text: Authored `docs/helix/01-frame/visuals/user-workflow.prompt.md` — the V10 user-workflow capstone prompt as a six-stage closed loop, matching the established sober/patent-figure register and DESIGN.md palette of the V8 tool prompts. Commit `cbb9d946` lands the prompt source; the generation half remains hard-blocked on the same artifact infrastructure that closed V6 (`ddx-ea3a7e23`) and V9 (`ddx-297e7b4b`) as `no_changes`, and the plan forbids manual fallback.
